commands.repair_reason: - [P1] This needs contributor or maintainer follow-up for branch refresh, real behavior proof, and a small sanitizer/changelog repair rather than an automated cleanup close.; Needs attention: The diff tightens a user-visible metadata boundary, but the new system-event matcher still leaves multiline continuation lines exposed.; Review finding: [P2] Strip continuation system-event lines тАФ src/auto-reply/reply/strip-in... (sha=e84ec68d6c2fda585020284cd1eada391ed6cea2)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Control UI grouped chat rendering and inbound metadata stripping so assistant scaffolding, system-event text, internal instruction lines, and metadata-only message groups are not shown to users.

PR surface: Source +119, Tests +125, Docs +4. Total +248 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main still renders grouped Control UI text from normalized content, lacks group-level empty suppression, and does not strip system-event/internal-instruction lines in `stripInboundMetadata`. I did not run a live UI reproduction because this review is read-only and no artifact proof was provided.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor or maintainer follow-up for branch refresh, real behavior proof, and a small sanitizer/changelog repair rather than an automated cleanup close.

target.title: Control UI + auto-reply: strip leaked agent scaffolding and metadata
target.body:
## Summary

Three related bugs where Control UI (and any user-facing surface that shares `stripInboundMetadata`) leaks message internals that should already have been stripped:

1. `renderGroupedMessage` was extracting text directly from `normalizedMessage.content` instead of going through `extractText`/`processMessageText`, so `<think>`/`<final>` scaffolding from Gemini-family models and inbound metadata sentinels were rendered verbatim in the chat bubble.
2. `stripInboundMetadata` only handled the structured JSON-block sentinels. Per-line `System:` / `System (untrusted):` event prefixes emitted by `session-system-events.ts`, and the paired "An async command you ran earlier has completed." / "Current time: тАж" internal agent instructions, still leaked into user-visible text.
3. With (1) and (2) fixed, user turns whose text consisted entirely of now-stripped metadata still rendered an orphan "You ┬╖ 12:34" bubble (avatar + sender label + timestamp + delete button, no content) because `renderMessageGroup` had no group-level suppression analogous to the per-message check inside `renderGroupedMessage`.

## Changes

- `ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.ts`: export `processMessageText` so other renderers can share the same per-role strip contract.
- `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts`:
  - route `renderGroupedMessage`'s text extraction through `processMessageText` (assistant тЖТ `stripThinkingTags`, user тЖТ `stripInboundMetadata(stripEnvelope(...))`, other тЖТ `stripEnvelope`).
  - add a group-level emptiness precheck in `renderMessageGroup` so a group whose inner messages all render empty returns `nothing` instead of leaving an orphan avatar/footer behind.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/strip-inbound-meta.ts`:
  - add `SYSTEM_EVENT_LINE_RE` and an `INTERNAL_INSTRUCTION_LINE_PREFIXES` list.
  - extend the fast-path `SENTINEL_FAST_RE` to include `System:` / `System (untrusted):` and the instruction prefixes so these variants still trigger the slow parse.
  - in the main loop, drop lines matching either pattern. Only line-anchored matches are stripped so prose like "Operating System: Linux" is preserved.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.test.ts` тАФ new `processMessageText` suite covers the empty-`<think></think>` + `<final>` variant I actually hit with `gemini-3-flash-preview`, plus the standard paired shape and plain pass-through for assistant/user roles.
- [x] `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/strip-inbound-meta.test.ts` тАФ new `per-line system events and internal instructions` suite covers consecutive `System (untrusted):` exec lines, trusted `System:` lines, the async-command / current-time instruction pair, and guards against stripping the inline "Operating System: Linux" prose variant.
- [x] `pnpm tsgo`, `pnpm check`, `pnpm check:import-cycles`, `pnpm check:madge-import-cycles` тАФ all green on the PR branch (run via `scripts/committer` pre-commit).
- [x] Manual smoke on a live self-hosted OpenClaw instance with Gemini 3 Flash Preview: `<final>тАж</final>` segments no longer render verbatim, `System (untrusted):` / async-command lines no longer surface in user bubbles, and orphan "You ┬╖ HH:MM" empty groups no longer appear after metadata-only turns. Existing `<think>` reasoning blocks still surface correctly in the dedicated reasoning panel.

No config schema, plugin-SDK surface, or build output shape was changed.
target.author: miyago9267

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.review_summary: The PR exports the chat text post-processor, updates grouped Control UI message rendering to strip assistant/user scaffolding and suppress all-empty groups, extends inbound metadata stripping for system-event/internal-instruction lines including the TUI leading stripper, and adds focused tests plus changelog entries.

target.title: Control UI + auto-reply: strip leaked agent scaffolding and metadata

- `src/auto-reply/reply/strip-inbound-meta.ts`:
